# Cronograma Interactivo — AI Agent Instructions

This is an interactive Gantt chart / schedule builder that runs entirely in the browser (static HTML + JS).
An AI agent can create and share schedules programmatically using the methods below.

---

## Option 1: Construct the share URL directly (no browser needed)

The share URL format is:

```
https://<host>/index.html#s=<base64(JSON.stringify(state))>
```

Where `base64` = `btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(state))))` in JavaScript,
or standard Base64 URL-safe encoding of the UTF-8 JSON string.

### Full State JSON Schema

```json
{
  "title": "My Schedule",
  "startMonth": "0",
  "endMonth": "11",
  "theme": "oscuro",
  "lang": "en",
  "projects": [
    {
      "name": "Project Name",
      "color": "#5B8CC8",
      "tasksByRow": [
        [
          {
            "name": "Task name",
            "startWeek": 1,
            "duration": 4,
            "isMilestone": false,
            "textPosition": "outside",
            "compact": false,
            "completed": false,
            "color": null
          }
        ]
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Field reference

| Field | Type | Values / Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | Schedule title shown in the header |
| `startMonth` | string (int 0–11) | 0=January, 11=December |
| `endMonth` | string (int 0–11) | Must be >= startMonth (same year assumed) |
| `theme` | string | `"oscuro"` `"claro"` `"moderno"` `"gris"` |
| `lang` | string | `"es"` (Spanish) or `"en"` (English) |
| `projects[].name` | string | Project group label |
| `projects[].color` | string | Hex color, e.g. `"#5B8CC8"` |
| `projects[].tasksByRow` | array of arrays | Each inner array = one visual row; tasks in same row overlap horizontally |
| `task.startWeek` | int (1-based) | Week 1 = first week of startMonth |
| `task.duration` | int | Duration in weeks |
| `task.isMilestone` | bool | `true` renders a diamond instead of a bar |
| `task.textPosition` | string | `"inside"` or `"outside"` |
| `task.compact` | bool | Uses smaller row height |
| `task.completed` | bool | Renders with a strikethrough style |
| `task.color` | string or null | Override color; `null` inherits project color |

### Color palette (default, cycle order)
`#5B8CC8` `#8A58B0` `#C97838` `#2E9E58` `#C8A028` `#B84848` `#2A9080` `#3A80B0`

### Minimal working example (JavaScript)

```javascript
const state = {
  title: "Q1 Roadmap",
  startMonth: "0",   // January
  endMonth: "5",     // June
  theme: "oscuro",
  lang: "en",
  projects: [
    {
      name: "Design",
      color: "#5B8CC8",
      tasksByRow: [
        [{ name: "Research", startWeek: 1, duration: 3, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Wireframes", startWeek: 4, duration: 4, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }]
      ]
    },
    {
      name: "Development",
      color: "#2E9E58",
      tasksByRow: [
        [{ name: "Backend", startWeek: 5, duration: 8, isMilestone: false, textPosition: "inside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }],
        [{ name: "Launch", startWeek: 13, duration: 1, isMilestone: true, textPosition: "outside", compact: false, completed: false, color: null }]
      ]
    }
  ]
};

const hash = btoa(unescape(encodeURIComponent(JSON.stringify(state))));
const url = `https://<host>/index.html#s=${hash}`;
// Share this URL — it opens the schedule directly in the browser.
```

---

## Option 2: Use the JavaScript API in a headless browser

When the page is loaded in a browser (or via Playwright / Puppeteer / browser-use), a global object is available:

```javascript
window.CronogramaAPI
```

### Methods

```javascript
// Read the current state
CronogramaAPI.getState()
// → { title, startMonth, endMonth, theme, lang, projects: [...] }

// Load a new schedule from a plain object or JSON string
CronogramaAPI.loadFromJSON(jsonOrStr)
// or
CronogramaAPI.setState({ title: "...", projects: [...], ... })

// Get the shareable URL (with #s= hash)
CronogramaAPI.getShareUrl()
// → "https://.../index.html#s=<base64>"

// Get a PNG image of the full schedule as a base64 data URL
await CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl()
// → "data:image/png;base64,..."
```

### Typical headless agent workflow

```javascript
// 1. Load the page
await page.goto('https://<host>/index.html');

// 2. Inject state
await page.evaluate((state) => {
  CronogramaAPI.loadFromJSON(state);
}, myScheduleJSON);

// 3. Get share URL
const url = await page.evaluate(() => CronogramaAPI.getShareUrl());

// 4. Get image
const dataUrl = await page.evaluate(async () => await CronogramaAPI.getImageDataUrl());
// dataUrl is "data:image/png;base64,..." — decode it to get the PNG bytes
```

---

## Option 3: URL autoexport parameters

Append `?autoexport=url` or `?autoexport=image` to the URL (combined with a `#s=` hash) to trigger
automatic actions on page load — useful for robots that can visit URLs.

### `?autoexport=url`
Injects a `<div id="autoexport-result">` at the top of the page containing the shareable URL as:
- Visible text (monospace bar at top)
- `data-share-url` attribute on the element

Example:
```
https://<host>/index.html?autoexport=url#s=<base64state>
```

The agent can then scrape `document.getElementById('autoexport-result').dataset.shareUrl`.

### `?autoexport=image`
After 600ms, downloads the PNG image automatically and places the data URL in:
- `document.getElementById('autoexport-result').dataset.imageDataUrl`
- `document.getElementById('autoexport-result').dataset.shareUrl`

---

## Option 4: DOM state element (always available)

After every render, the element `#app-test-state` is updated with:

```javascript
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.shareUrl  // current share URL
document.getElementById('app-test-state').dataset.state     // full state JSON string
```

This lets any DOM-scraping agent read the current state and URL at any time without calling JS functions.

---

## Notes

- All months are 0-indexed (0 = January, 11 = December).
- `startWeek` is 1-indexed: week 1 is the first week of `startMonth`.
- Tasks in the same inner array of `tasksByRow` are rendered on the same horizontal row and can overlap.
- The schedule renders entirely client-side with no server calls required.
- The app auto-saves state to `localStorage` between sessions.
